Strategy Name: Return Asymmetry Effect in Commodity Futures
Strategy Description: A long-short strategy in commodity futures based on a distribution-based asymmetry measure (IE).
Core Logic: Investors overvalue assets with high upside potential (high IE) and undervalue those with high downside potential. Going long on low IE and short on high IE commodities captures this mispricing.
Entry Conditions: Calculate IE = (days return > avg + 2std) - (days return < avg - 2std) over the last 260 days for 22 commodity futures. Long the bottom 7 (lowest IE) and short the top 7 (highest IE).
Indicators Used: Daily returns, Standard deviation, IE measure.
Risk Management Rules: Equally weighted; uses a universe of 22 liquid commodity futures.
Long/Short Logic: Long bottom 7, short top 7 of IE ranking.
Market Regime Suitability: Yes; serves as a hedge for stocks due to negative correlation with the S&P 500.
Full Notes: The asymmetry effect is distinct from the skewness effect, providing additional diversification benefits.
Mathematical/Statistical Concepts: Distribution-based asymmetry (IE), Empirical distribution, Standard deviation thresholds.
